name: How long does a kitchen remodel usually take in San Mateo County?
headline: How long does a kitchen remodel usually take in San Mateo County?

mainEntity.name: How long does a kitchen remodel usually take in San Mateo County?
mainEntity.text: How long does a kitchen remodel usually take in San Mateo County?

acceptedAnswer.text: For most homes in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and Half Moon Bay, a **typical kitchen remodel takes about 4 to 8 weeks of on-site work**, once design and permits are done. Smaller, straightforward remodels (no walls moved, minimal electrical and plumbing changes) tend toward the shorter side. If you’re changing the floor plan, removing or altering walls, or doing significant structural or electrical work, expect closer to **8–12 weeks**. Remember that this does **not** include the planning phase. Designing the new kitchen, selecting materials, and getting permits can take several weeks on its own, especially if your city’s building department is busy or if your home is older and needs more detailed plans. Delays often happen for a few reasons: - Materials arriving late (custom cabinets, special tile, or appliances). - Hidden problems inside walls, like old wiring, dry rot, or subfloor damage. - Changes you request after work starts. To avoid surprises, ask your contractor for a written schedule with key milestones: demo, framing, rough plumbing/electrical, inspections, drywall, cabinets, counters, finishes. Plan for a period when you may not be able to cook normally; many homeowners set up a temporary mini-kitchen in another room to make life easier while work is underway.
